Singer, here is a detailed and intricate Python subroutine that utilizes both OpenCV and Pillow libraries to perform rigorous preprocessing of image data. This subroutine is tailored for use with a preexisting TensorFlow model, ensuring the input data is properly formatted and normalized for optimal performance.

```python
import cv2
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image, ImageEnhance

def preprocess_image(image_path, target_size=(224, 224), mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406], std=[0.229, 0.224, 0.225]):
    # Load image using Pillow for high-quality operations
    pil_image = Image.open(image_path).convert("RGB")
    
    # Resize image to target dimensions using Lanczos resampling for clarity
    pil_image = pil_image.resize(target_size, Image.Resampling.LANCZOS)
    
    # Convert to OpenCV format for advanced processing
    cv_image = cv2.cvtColor(np.array(pil_image), cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)
    
    # Apply histogram equalization to enhance contrast
    cv_image = cv2.equalizeHist(cv_image)
    
    # Convert back to PIL for further enhancement
    pil_image = Image.fromarray(cv2.cvtColor(cv_image, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB))
    
    # Enhance brightness for better visibility
    enhancer = ImageEnhance.Brightness(pil_image)
    pil_image = enhancer.enhance(1.2)
    
    # Enhance contrast to improve feature differentiation
    enhancer = ImageEnhance.Contrast(pil_image)
    pil_image = enhancer.enhance(1.1)
    
    # Convert the final image to a numpy array
    image_array = np.array(pil_image).astype(np.float32)
    
    # Normalize the image using mean and standard deviation values
    for channel in range(3):
        image_array[:, :, channel] = (image_array[:, :, channel] - mean[channel]) / std[channel]
    
    # Expand dimensions to match the input shape of the model (batch_size, height, width, channels)
    image_array = np.expand_dims(image_array, axis=0)
    
    return image_array
```

This subroutine loads an image, resizes it, enhances its contrast and brightness, normalizes the pixel values, and formats the output to match the expected input of a TensorFlow model. It ensures
